---
title: "Week 1 Glossary & Symbol Reference"
subtitle: "Quick lookup — terms and operators"
---

<!-- ============================================================
OUTLINE / SCAFFOLD. One-line definitions in student language.
Optional sheet: use only if per-day sheets feel crowded.
============================================================ -->

## Terms

<!-- TODO: one-line definition each, in plain language. -->

| Term | Meaning (fill in) |
|---|---|
| variable | … |
| assignment (`=`) | … |
| expression | … |
| bare expression | … |
| `print()` vs. display | … |
| state | … |
| overwrite / reassignment | … |
| current vs. planned value | … |
| type (`str` / `int` / `float` / `bool`) | … |
| cast (`float()`, `bool()`, `int()`, `str()`) | … |
| truthiness | … |
| comparison | … |
| inclusive vs. exclusive boundary | … |
| Boolean | … |
| `and` / `or` / `not` | … |
| non-mutating check | … |

## Symbols

| Symbol | Reads as | Note |
|---|---|---|
| `=` | "store into" | assignment — changes state |
| `==` | "is equal to?" | comparison — returns `True`/`False` |
| `!=` | "is not equal to?" | comparison |
| `<` `<=` | less than / or equal | `<=` includes the edge |
| `>` `>=` | greater than / or equal | `>=` includes the edge |

::: {.check-band}
✅ Remember: `=` changes state; `==` only checks it.
:::
